title: sidalcea neomexicana subsp. parviflora
intro: Sidalcea neomexicana subsp. parviflora, also known as New Mexican checkerbloom, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Sidalcea neomexicana subsp. parviflora include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of allergies and colds. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
